notes:
vô lớp = go into the classroom / go to class (physically enter the class).

Vô means “go in / enter”, and lớp is “classroom” or “class.” It’s used when you talk about physically going inside the class, not just attending school in general.

In the North, people usually say vào lớp instead of vô lớp. Same meaning — just a regional difference.
exampleSentences: Sáng nay em dậy muộn nên phải chạy nhanh để vô lớp.
Anh nhớ mang sách vở khi vô lớp học tiếng Anh nhé.
Chị thường gặp bạn bè ở cổng trường trước khi vô lớp.
notes_md:
**vô lớp** = go into the classroom / go to class (physically enter the class).

**Vô** means “go in / enter”, and **lớp** is “classroom” or “class.” It’s used when you talk about physically going inside the class, not just attending school in general.


In the North, people usually say **vào lớp** instead of **vô lớp**. Same meaning — just a regional difference.
exampleSentencesTranslated: This morning I woke up late so I had to run fast to go to class.
Remember to bring your books when you go to class for English, okay?
She usually meets her friends at the school gate before she goes to class.

definitions.vi: Cụm động từ chỉ hành động đi vào trong phòng học để bắt đầu tiết học.
definitions.en: Go to class; enter the classroom.

example.es: Date prisa para entrar a clase.

content.sentence: Sáng nay em dậy muộn nên phải chạy nhanh để ___.

translation.en: This morning I woke up late so I had to run fast to go to class.

content.sentence: Anh nhớ mang sách vở khi ___ học tiếng Anh nhé.

translation.en: Remember to bring your books when you go to class for English, okay?
